title: Glossa
description: Cross-project translation management — REST API + Lit web components + own ICU subset. See README.md and docs/design.md for the full architecture.

features.title: ICU subset (@felixgeelhaar/glossa-format)
features.description:
Own ICU MessageFormat subset, ~200 LOC + ~150 LOC tests. Lands first because nothing else depends on it.

Scope:
- Variable interpolation `{name}`
- Plural `{count, plural, one {…} other {…}}` backed by Intl.PluralRules
- Selection `{gender, select, female {…} male {…} other {…}}`
- Nested combinations
- Apostrophe escaping per ICU spec (`'{'` literal)
- Defer number/date/relative-time/list to Intl.* built-ins

Acceptance:
- Vitest suite covering ICU spec edge cases (negative counts, apostrophe literals, deeply nested selects)
- Bundle size <3kb gzipped
- Zero external runtime dependencies
- Exports a `format(message, locale, values)` function + a `parse(message)` AST helper for the admin lint pass

features.title: Go API + Postgres schema (apps/api)
features.description:
REST + SSE service backing the admin UI and the Lit components. Mirrors Brotwerk's apps/api hex-arch (domain → app → interfaces → infra).

Library picks (locked):
- HTTP: gin-gonic/gin (matches Brotwerk)
- DB: pgx/v5 + sqlc-generated query types
- Migrations: golang-migrate
- Logging: felixgeelhaar/bolt slog handler
- Resilience: felixgeelhaar/fortify — ratelimit, circuitbreaker, timeout, retry, ferrors for typed errors
- Auth: golang-jwt/jwt for admin sessions; SHA-256-hashed API keys for consumer/CLI traffic

Scope:
- golang-migrate migrations for tenants, projects, locales, keys, translations, audit_log, users (schema per docs/design.md §6)
- sqlc-generated repos for each table
- Domain layer: Project, Locale, Key, Translation, Tenant value objects
- Use cases: CreateProject, UpsertKeys (CLI scan), UpdateTranslation, ListBundle, RotateAPIKey
- REST endpoints per docs/design.md §5.1, served via gin
- JWT auth middleware (admin), API-key middleware (consumer/CLI), fortify ratelimit per-tenant
- Row-Level Security policies enforcing tenant_id isolation (cross-tenant read impossible at DB layer)
- bolt-backed slog handler for structured logs
- /healthz + /readyz

Acceptance:
- `go test ./...` clean with table-driven coverage on each use case
- Integration test against a real Postgres (testcontainers or docker compose) covering RLS isolation
- OpenAPI spec generated from handlers, committed to api/openapi.yaml

features.title: SSE live-update channel
features.description:
Translator edits a key in the admin UI → consumer apps see the new value within seconds without a deploy. Server-Sent Events because consumer side is browser-first, no need for full WebSocket bidirectionality.

Scope:
- API endpoint `GET /api/v1/projects/:slug/sse` (per docs/design.md §5.1)
- Event envelope `{type: "translation.updated", project, locale, key, value, status}` per §5.2
- Server-side fanout: in-process map per project for MVP; Redis pubsub when multi-replica
- Client-side reconnection with exponential backoff in @felixgeelhaar/glossa-sdk
- Heartbeat every 30s so corporate proxies don't kill the connection
- Per-tenant rate limit on translator broadcasts (avoid one project spamming all subscribers)

Acceptance:
- End-to-end test: edit a translation in admin → assert SSE event lands in a subscribing consumer within 1s
- Connection survives a 60s idle window
- Reconnection recovers missed events using a `Last-Event-ID` cursor

features.title: Glossa SDK (@felixgeelhaar/glossa-sdk)
features.description:
HTTP fetch + in-memory cache + SSE subscription, framework-agnostic. The boundary the Lit components and CLI both consume; owns network code so the components stay pure rendering.

Scope:
- `Client(config)` factory: project, locale, apiUrl, apiKey
- `client.bundle(locale)` returns a Promise<Record<string, string>> with etag + If-None-Match handling
- `client.subscribe(onEvent, onError)` opens SSE, auto-reconnects with backoff
- In-memory bundle cache keyed by `(project, locale)`; invalidated on SSE updates
- `client.scan(keys)` for the CLI side (batch upsert)
- Pluggable transport for tests (mock-fetch friendly)

Acceptance:
- Vitest covering happy path, 304 responses, SSE reconnect, key miss → undefined return
- Bundle size <5kb gzipped (no axios/superagent — native fetch)
- Works in Node (CLI) and browser (components)

features.title: Lit web components (@felixgeelhaar/glossa-elements)
features.description:
Framework-agnostic translation rendering. `<glossa-provider>` boots one SDK client; descendant `<glossa-text>` / `<glossa-rich>` / `<glossa-plural>` / `<glossa-select>` reactively render translations.

Scope:
- `<glossa-provider project locale api-url api-key strict>` — root; exposes context via a ReactiveController
- `<glossa-text key>` — flat string lookup; slot content is fallback when key missing
- `<glossa-rich key vars>` — ICU MessageFormat via @felixgeelhaar/glossa-format
- `<glossa-plural key count>` — convenience wrapper around rich for the most common case
- `<glossa-select key value>` — gender/select wrapper
- Locale switching: provider's `locale` attribute change triggers re-render of every descendant
- SSE event propagation: updates flow into a Lit signal that all components subscribe to
- A11y: rendered text inherits parent element semantics (no extra wrapper that breaks landmarks)
- Strict mode (`<glossa-provider strict>`) logs missing keys to console.warn in dev

Acceptance:
- Demo page in apps/admin that mounts every component on the same provider; toggling locale swaps every visible string
- Component tests using @open-wc/testing or vitest+jsdom for slot fallback, missing-key behaviour, locale switch
- Total package bundle size <10kb gzipped

features.title: CLI (@felixgeelhaar/glossa-cli)
features.description:
Build-time integration: scan source for keys, pull/push bundles. Doesn't own runtime behaviour — the SSE channel covers that.

Scope:
- `glossa init` — scaffolds a `glossa.config.{ts,json}` with project slug, locales, api-url, scan globs
- `glossa scan` — walks source matching the config globs, extracts `<glossa-text key="…">` usages and matching template-literal patterns, POSTs to `/keys:scan` as a batch upsert
- `glossa pull` — fetches all locale bundles to disk for build-time baking
- `glossa push <bundle.json>` — pushes a translated bundle (translator-export workflow)
- Exit codes: 0 success, 1 config error, 2 network error, 3 partial scan failure
- Honours `.gitignore` when walking source (nox-style — avoid scanning node_modules)

Acceptance:
- Tested against a fixture project tree in the repo; `glossa scan` finds keys correctly
- `pull` writes deterministic JSON output (stable key order) so the diff is meaningful
- Standalone npm install: `npx @felixgeelhaar/glossa-cli scan` works without a global install

features.title: Admin UI (apps/admin)
features.description:
Translator interface. Lit + Vite, dogfoods @felixgeelhaar/glossa-elements + @felixgeelhaar/glossa-sdk so changes to the public packages get exercised every time the admin is touched.

Scope:
- Login (JWT) + project switcher
- Key list per (project, locale) with status filters (untranslated / needs-review / approved)
- Edit form per key — supports the ICU subset with a live preview rendering via @felixgeelhaar/glossa-format
- Bulk import / export JSON
- Diff view: "untranslated keys vs needs-review" snapshot
- Project management: locale CRUD, API-key rotation, audit log view
- Per-user access: scope a translator to specific locales (`users.locales[]` column)

Acceptance:
- A translator can sign in, pick a project, edit a key, save, see status flip to "pending review", and the SSE event lands in the demo consumer
- A11y: keyboard-only key edit + save flow works end-to-end
- Bundle size budget: <250kb gzipped for the admin SPA

features.title: Deployment (k3s on edge-1)
features.description:
Mirror IRI/Brotwerk's k3s deploy patterns — single binary API + static admin SPA + Postgres StatefulSet.

Scope:
- `apps/api/Dockerfile` (distroless final stage), `apps/admin/Dockerfile` (nginx serving the static build)
- GHA workflow `.github/workflows/release.yml` that builds + pushes `ghcr.io/felixgeelhaar/glossa-api` and `glossa-admin` on tag push, then patches the k3s deployments
- `deploy/k3s/` manifests: Postgres StatefulSet (20Gi PVC), api + admin Deployments + Services, Ingress with cert-manager TLS, ConfigMap + Secret (API keys, JWT signing key)
- Daily backup CronJob via the existing rclone-config + Hetzner Storage Box pattern
- Migration init-container on the API pod, same shape as Brotwerk

Acceptance:
- `git tag v0.1.0 && git push --tags` → release workflow ships both images and rolls them out
- Admin reachable at the chosen subdomain over HTTPS with cert-manager-issued cert
- Postgres survives a pod restart (PVC retained); backup target receives a daily dump

features.title: First consumer migration (IRI or Brotwerk)
features.description:
Validate the whole stack against a real consumer. Pick whichever sister project is calmer at the time — IRI has 170+ hard-coded German strings, Brotwerk has fewer but a richer i18n already (de/en JSON bundles).

Scope:
- Add `<glossa-provider>` to the consumer's root layout
- Replace 5–10 representative hardcoded strings with `<glossa-text>` / `<glossa-rich>` to prove the end-to-end loop
- Run `glossa scan` from the consumer's CI to seed Glossa with the key list
- Wire a fallback: if Glossa is unreachable, the consumer's pre-existing i18n JSON wins (don't take a hard dependency on Glossa availability for the first migration)

Acceptance:
- Editing a translation in the Glossa admin updates the consumer within 5s without a redeploy
- Consumer build still works when Glossa is offline (verified by killing the API pod during a build)
- One translator can update at least one key end-to-end without engineering involvement

features.title: Instrument onboarding analytics events
features.description:
Closes the build-trap signal flagged in the expert review: every feature shipped so far ties to no metric. Wire five named events that capture the cold-start funnel for a brand-new operator:

1. project_created — admin POSTs /admin/projects successfully
2. first_key_synced — first translationkey upsert on a project (via CLI scan or bulk import)
3. first_translation_edited — first PATCH /translations on a project
4. first_consumer_request — first authed GET /messages on a project
5. first_ai_translation — first ai_translated row written by the fan-out worker

Events emitted server-side from the existing use cases (zero new dependencies). Store in a new audit_log-adjacent `analytics_events` table or fold into audit_log with a new event_kind column. The metric this unlocks: time-to-first-approved-translation per project, which is the proxy for whether onboarding works. Surface a /admin/metrics endpoint (admin-only) that returns the cohort funnel.

features.title: Define ECP + positioning doc
features.description: Critical Dunford/Voje finding. Today Glossa is positioned for 'Felix + three projects'; the next 100 users are someone else. Pick one ECP (small EU SaaS founders self-hosting the EU-sovereign stack, OR German-language indie devs avoiding Crowdin pricing) and lock the positioning brief: best-fit customer, alternatives, differentiated capabilities, differentiated value, category. Output as docs/positioning.md and reflect in README hero copy + landing GHCR description.

features.title: Run second-user usability test
features.description: The single highest-leverage validation: watch one human who is NOT Felix complete a 10-key German→English translation cold-start. Goal: capture friction points in the first 15 minutes (login → first key → first approved translation rendering on a consumer page). Pre-write a 5-question script; record screen + audio with consent; transcribe findings into docs/usability-2026-Q2.md. Output: prioritized list of blockers + at least one go/no-go decision on next feature investment.

features.title: Project switcher polish (hide when N=1, show name on first paint)
features.description: Hick's Law violation. Hide the project switcher entirely when projects.length === 1; show the project name inline next to the tenant identity. When N>1, fix the first-paint timing so the trigger shows the active project's name immediately (Lit-select option timing bug — pass options before value, or render gl-select only after activeProject is non-null).

features.title: Bulk approve: progress UI + undo window
features.description: Doherty Threshold + Krug undo>confirmation. Replace the silent 'Approving…' label with a live counter (`12 / 200 approved…`) updated per row. Add a 5-second 'Undo' toast that delays the actual PATCH calls; clicking Undo aborts before any side effects fire. Covers the accidental 'select all → approve' footgun without nagging the user with a modal on every bulk action.

features.title: Editor keyboard shortcuts (j/k/a/r)
features.description: Translators editing 200+ keys live in the editor all day. Add: j (next key), k (previous key), Enter (open edit), Esc (close edit), a (approve current), r (mark needs_review), / (focus search). Show a keyboard help dialog via ? key. Setting toggle (default on for admins, off for translators until they ask). Persist preference per-user in users table via /admin/users/:id/preferences PATCH.

features.title: AI provider: validate key inline on save
features.description: Today the Test button surfaces auth errors AFTER the row lands in DB. Change handleCreateAIProvider to optionally trigger a single Translate call (with a tiny known-good source like 'Hello') BEFORE returning success; if it fails, return 422 with the upstream error and don't persist. Add `?validate=true` query param on the POST so the existing 'add without test' path stays available for power users who want to plant a row for later editing.

features.title: SSR/hydration uncertainty UX in glossa-elements
features.description: AI UX pattern: fallback content shouldn't look identical to live content. Today `<glossa-text>` slot content renders the same before + after hydration; users can't tell what's source-of-truth vs SSR cache. Add a subtle visual treatment until the provider's first bundle resolves: italic font-style OR a 1px dotted underline OR an aria-busy attribute paired with a CSS opacity 0.9. Trigger off the provider's context value (version 0 = pre-hydration).

features.title: Translator invite + first-login flow
features.description: Today the only path to a translator account is admin creates user with a known password. Replace with invitation tokens: admin clicks 'Invite' → backend mints a single-use invite token (expires in 48h) → admin shares the URL out-of-band (link, email, Slack) → translator follows link → sets password → lands in /editor on the locales they're scoped to. New tables: invites (id, tenant_id, email, role, locales, token_hash, expires_at, accepted_at). Two new endpoints: POST /admin/invites (create), POST /auth/accept-invite (consume). Email integration is OUT OF SCOPE for v1 — link sharing is manual.

features.title: Audit log: filter UI (actor kind, date range, key/value search)
features.description: Data model already supports actor_kind ('user' | 'ai' | 'system') + actor_label + timestamps. UI is unfiltered. Add chip filters for actor_kind, a date-range picker (last 1d / 7d / 30d / custom), and a substring search on before_value/after_value. Server-side: extend ListAuditForTenant with optional filter params; keep the existing pagination story. Critical for compliance reviews + post-incident forensics.

features.title: Microcopy + footer polish
features.description: Small Krug-style sweep: (1) lead empty-state body with the action verb ('Run `glossa scan`...' instead of 'Add translation keys by scanning...'); (2) revoke confirm names the consequence concretely ('Any consumer using this key will stop working immediately'); (3) bulk approve confirm always shows the count ('Approve 47 keys?'); (4) add a colophon strip with `glossa v0.1.x · github · docs` so users can find help.

features.title: Accessibility sweep: aria-busy, aria-live, focus management
features.description: (1) Bulk approve sets aria-busy=true on the selection bar during the serial loop. (2) The 'X selected' counter wraps in aria-live='polite' so screen readers announce changes. (3) After dismissing the reveal-once key panel, focus returns to the toolbar button that opened the flow. (4) Editor row checkbox: header column gets a visually-hidden 'Select' label. (5) gl-tabs current-tab announce via aria-selected (verify already there; add if not). Test pass with VoiceOver + axe-core in CI.

features.title: Three moments of delight (Walter)
features.description: Lift Glossa from usable → pleasurable with three small wins: (1) First-approval celebration toast when a project's first key flips to 'approved' — links to the consumer SDK snippet. (2) AI fan-out rows show a subtle violet pulse while the agent is mid-flight; settle to the ai_translated badge when done. (3) Copy-API-key button briefly says 'Copied' + input border flashes violet (Doherty + Gestalt closure). Each is <30 lines. Land all three behind a single 'micro-interactions on/off' setting in case translators find them distracting.

features.title: Diff view → Now / Next / Later columns
features.description: Today /diff is a flat table of (locale, total, pending, needs_review, approved). Reframe as a per-locale Now/Next/Later kanban (Ant Murphy): Now = pending + ai_translated (action needed), Next = needs_review (queued), Later = approved (done). Click a column header to filter the editor by that status. Visual story matches what reviewers actually do: 'work the Now column down'.

features.title: CLI install snippet: package-manager tabs
features.description: Editor empty-state hardcodes `pnpx`. Replace with a small tab UI that shows the install command for npm / pnpm / bun / yarn so users running any of the four don't bounce off the snippet. Three lines per tab, same shape. Use gl-tabs primitive so we get keyboard nav for free.

features.title: Read-only API key for brotwerk-prod (ECP swap)
features.description: Operational follow-up to the scoped-keys feature. Today brotwerk uses the legacy write-scope key inherited from migration 0003. Issue a new 'read'-scope key via the admin UI ('brotwerk web' label), swap into brotwerk apps/web/.env + the CI build args, redeploy brotwerk, then revoke the legacy write key. Validates the read-only path end-to-end + closes the embedded-write-key security gap flagged earlier.
